name: Vecchiarelle ( Honey-Coated Fritters )
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour onto a work surface and make a well in the center.
2. Add the yeast and salt to the well, then mix with fingertips.
3. Gradually add lukewarm water while mixing until a smooth, pliable dough forms.
4. Shape the dough into a ball, sprinkle with a little flour, and cover with a damp cloth.
5. Allow the dough to rise in a warm place for about 1 hour or until doubled in size.
6. Knead the risen dough on a floured surface.
7. Roll out the dough thinly and cut into small sticks, approximately 1/2 inch thick and 2 1/2 inches long.
8. Heat oil in a deep-fat fryer to frying temperature.
9. Fry the sticks, a few at a time, until golden brown.
10. Drain on absorbent kitchen paper and keep warm while frying the remaining sticks.
11. In a saucepan, gently heat the honey until it melts, stirring constantly.
12. Dip the fried sticks into the melted honey to coat.
13. Serve immediately.
Serves 4-6
